name: German Soft Pretzels
recipeIngredient: 6 tablespoons Baking soda in 6 cups Water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water.
2. Add 1 1/2 cups of the flour, salad oil, and sugar to the yeast mixture. Beat for about 3 minutes to make a smooth batter.
3. Gradually stir in enough of the remaining flour until a soft dough forms.
4. Turn out onto a floured board and knead until smooth and satiny (about 5 minutes), adding flour as needed to prevent sticking.
5. Place dough in a greased bowl; turn over to grease the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place until double (about 1 hour).
6. Punch down the dough, turn out onto a floured board, and divide into 12 pieces.
7. Shape each piece into a smooth ball by gently kneading.
8. Roll each into a smooth rope about 18 inches long, then twist into a pretzel shape.
9. Place slightly apart on a greased baking sheet with loose ends underneath. Let rise, uncovered, until puffy (about 25 minutes).
10. Meanwhile, in a 3-quart stainless steel or enameled pan (not aluminum), bring soda water to a boil and adjust the water level to keep it gently boiling.
11. Lower 1 pretzel at a time into the pan using a slotted spatula.
12. Let simmer for 10 seconds on each side, then lift from the water, drain briefly on the spatula, and return to the baking sheet.
13. Let dry briefly before sprinkling with coarse salt and letting stand, uncovered, until all have been simmered.
14. Bake in a preheated 425°F oven for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden brown.
15. Transfer to racks; serve warm with butter, mustard, or cream cheese. Alternatively, let cool completely, wrap airtight, and freeze. To reheat, place frozen on ungreased baking sheets and bake in a preheated 400°F oven for about 10 minutes or until hot. Makes 1 dozen pretzels.
